issues.description: The script uses GM_xmlhttpRequest to send requests to third-party servers including sponsor.ajay.app, co.wuk.sh, cobalt.api.timelessnesses.me, and api.cobalt.tools. These requests may include user data or usage statistics related to SponsorBlock segments and video downloads.
issues.recommendation: Ensure that no sensitive personal data is sent in requests. Provide transparency about data usage and obtain user consent if necessary.

issues.description: The script reads and writes persistent settings using GM_setValue and GM_getValue but does not appear to access document.cookie, localStorage, or sessionStorage, nor does it listen to keyboard events or access form inputs.
issues.location: Code analysis of storage and event listeners
issues.recommendation: No action needed as no privacy-invasive data collection detected.

issues.description: No use of eval(), new Function(), setTimeout(string), or dynamic script injection detected in the provided code snippet. No @require directives loading remote scripts found.
issues.recommendation: Continue to avoid dynamic code execution from untrusted sources.

issues.description: The script requests GM_xmlhttpRequest, GM_setValue, GM_getValue, and GM_addStyle permissions, all of which are used appropriately in the code. No excessive or unused permissions detected.
No usage of sensitive APIs such as navigator.geolocation, RTCPeerConnection, MediaDevices, or Clipboard API detected in the provided code snippet.
No evidence of code obfuscation, base64 decoding, or suspicious string concatenation detected in the provided code snippet.
issues.recommendation: Maintain code transparency for security and maintainability.

issues.description: The script does not use @require to load external libraries; all external dependencies are accessed via GM_xmlhttpRequest to known domains. The domains appear to be related to the script author or known services. No version pinning applies as no external libraries are loaded via @require.
issues.recommendation: Verify the trustworthiness of connected domains and monitor for supply chain risks.

script_name: EasyTube V3 — Ad Skip, SponsorBlock & HD Download⬇️🚀
summary: The script performs network requests to third-party servers for SponsorBlock API and video download services, which constitutes data transmission risk. However, it does not collect sensitive user data such as cookies, form inputs, or keyboard events. No remote code execution or permission abuse detected. Overall, the script poses a medium security risk primarily due to data transmission to external servers.
